claim: Apply the default shift habit: before doing any task, ask 'how could AI do this or at least 30% of it' instead of assuming you must do it manually
actionable: For every repetitive or boring task this week, pause and ask your AI agent how it could handle 30-75% of the work.
source_title: Build & Sell Claude Code Operating Systems (2+ Hour Course)

claim: Break down large automation goals into tiny reusable chunks rather than trying to automate entire workflows at once; each small chunk can be reused in different contexts
actionable: Map out a complex task (like video production), identify the atomic sub-tasks (ideation, scripting, thumbnail design), and automate each separately.
source_title: Build & Sell Claude Code Operating Systems (2+ Hour Course)

claim: Treat AI as a mentor, not a vending machine: ask 'why' about AI-generated code or outputs, understand edge cases, and verify it works with unusual inputs
actionable: When Claude generates a script for you, ask follow-up questions about what happens if inputs are empty, null, or malformed before deploying.
source_title: Build & Sell Claude Code Operating Systems (2+ Hour Course)

claim: Every task has a leverage percentage (0-100%) for AI; the question is never 'will AI do this for me' but 'to what extent can I leverage AI here (30%, 60%, etc.)'
actionable: Estimate the AI leverage percentage for your top 5 weekly tasks; pursue those above 30% first.
source_title: Build & Sell Claude Code Operating Systems (2+ Hour Course)

claim: Expect a 20% productivity dip when first implementing an AI OS; break-even occurs around day 3-5 due to exponential learning curve, then gains compound (5-10x by week 2)
actionable: When onboarding an AI OS, commit to at least a 2-week trial before deciding if ROI is worth it; don't abandon during the dip phase.
source_title: Build & Sell Claude Code Operating Systems (2+ Hour Course)

claim: Context is what the AI knows about you: your business, voice, priorities, team, finances; without strong context the AI answers like a stranger rather than an executive assistant
actionable: Create context files for your AIOS covering: company overview, your communication style (paste 2-3 real writing samples), quarterly priorities, financial status, and team structure.
source_title: Build & Sell Claude Code Operating Systems (2+ Hour Course)

claim: Connections are pathways to data: connect AIOS to tools via APIs, MCP servers, or CLI tools (Google Workspace CLI is especially powerful); without connections AI has no access to business data beyond the web
actionable: Audit your top 7 data sources (revenue, customers, calendar, comms, tasks, meetings, knowledge), then wire each via API key or MCP to your Claude Code project.
source_title: Build & Sell Claude Code Operating Systems (2+ Hour Course)

claim: Capabilities are reusable markdown-based recipes (skills) that encode your SOPs; once written and stored in .claude/skills, they can be invoked repeatedly with consistent, predictable outputs
actionable: Identify 3 weekly processes you repeat, document them as step-by-step SOPs, then convert each into a skill.md file with front matter (name, description) and workflow steps.
source_title: Build & Sell Claude Code Operating Systems (2+ Hour Course)

claim: Cadence is when your AIOS acts on its own via scheduled routines (local or cloud-based); cloud routines run on Anthropic's infrastructure while your laptop is off, enabling 24/7 automation
actionable: Set up one cloud routine for a weekly task (e.g., Monday morning planning) using claude.ai/code or the desktop app's scheduled tasks feature.
source_title: Build & Sell Claude Code Operating Systems (2+ Hour Course)

claim: Create separate API accounts (e.g., 'MyAI' in ClickUp) for your AIOS rather than using your personal account; enables granular permissions, cost attribution per agent, and reduced risk from AI mistakes
actionable: For each tool connected to your AIOS (ClickUp, Google, Slack, etc.), create a separate service account with read-only or limited write permissions.
source_title: Build & Sell Claude Code Operating Systems (2+ Hour Course)

claim: Cloud Code uses three-level progressive context loading: (1) search skill names/descriptions only (100 tokens), (2) load full skill.md if matched (1000-2000 tokens), (3) load supporting files only if needed; keeps token costs low
actionable: Keep skill.md files under 500 lines and move detailed references to separate files; link them explicitly in your skill.
source_title: Build & Sell Claude Code Operating Systems (2+ Hour Course)

claim: Skills improve through iterative feedback: invoke skill, observe output, provide corrections, let AI fix the skill; by run 5-10, skill quality is dramatically better than the initial version
actionable: When building a new skill, run it 5-10 times with the same input, give feedback after each run ('fix the spacing', 'use brand colors', 'add the logo'), and let Claude update skill.md automatically.
source_title: Build & Sell Claude Code Operating Systems (2+ Hour Course)

claim: For data that never changes (list IDs, team member names, endpoints), hardcode them directly in skill.md rather than requiring API lookups every time; saves tokens and latency
actionable: If your ClickUp skill always queries the same 3 lists, add their IDs directly to the skill workflow step instead of asking the skill to search them each time.
source_title: Build & Sell Claude Code Operating Systems (2+ Hour Course)

claim: Google Workspace CLI gives a single unified interface to all Google tools (Drive, Docs, Sheets, Gmail, Calendar); auto-discovers new APIs and avoids token overhead vs multiple MCP servers
actionable: Install Google Workspace CLI in your Claude Code project, authenticate via OAuth, and use it for all Google-based automations instead of separate API calls.
source_title: Build & Sell Claude Code Operating Systems (2+ Hour Course)

claim: Google Workspace CLI includes 100+ pre-built recipes (multi-step workflows) for common tasks like creating presentations from data, auto-tagging emails by priority, scheduling meetings from sheets
actionable: Before building a custom GWS skill, check the GWS documentation for built-in recipes that may already solve your problem.
source_title: Build & Sell Claude Code Operating Systems (2+ Hour Course)

claim: Cloud routines run on Anthropic's infrastructure and work with laptop off; local scheduled tasks require your machine on; cloud routines are 1-hour minimum frequency, up to 15/day on Max plan
actionable: For automations that must run 24/7, use cloud routines; for more frequent or laptop-dependent tasks, use local scheduled tasks.
source_title: Build & Sell Claude Code Operating Systems (2+ Hour Course)

claim: Cloud routines cannot access .env files (not pushed to GitHub); store API keys in cloud environment variables within the routine config; explicitly tell the routine to read from environment, not .env
actionable: When setting up a cloud routine, add all API keys to the cloud environment config, then in your prompt write 'use the API key from the environment variable, not .env'.
source_title: Build & Sell Claude Code Operating Systems (2+ Hour Course)

claim: Each cloud routine run is stateless and ephemeral: GitHub repo is cloned, task runs, then destroyed; routines cannot rely on cookies, local files, or browser state persisting between runs
actionable: If your routine needs authentication, use API keys or headers instead of cookies; if it needs browser state, use local scheduled tasks instead.
source_title: Build & Sell Claude Code Operating Systems (2+ Hour Course)

claim: Cloud routines have 3 network access levels: 'trusted' (Anthropic-verified services only, safer), 'full' (all domains, required for some tools like ClickUp, higher risk), 'custom' (allow specific domains)
actionable: Start with 'trusted' mode; only upgrade to 'full' if necessary. Use 'custom' to allow specific external APIs.
source_title: Build & Sell Claude Code Operating Systems (2+ Hour Course)

claim: Cloud routines must have one-shot prompts that never need user interaction; design routines to assume autonomous execution with no permission prompts or clarifications
actionable: When writing a cloud routine prompt, avoid questions like 'which list?' or 'should I approve this?'; hardcode all decisions and make the routine deterministic.
source_title: Build & Sell Claude Code Operating Systems (2+ Hour Course)

claim: Store your AIOS as a GitHub repo (private OK) to enable syncing across devices and cloud execution; use .gitignore to exclude .env; organize with .claude/skills, context/, references/, decisions/
actionable: Initialize your AIOS in a GitHub repo with .gitignore excluding .env; sync your .claude, context, and skills folders; share the GitHub URL in cloud routine configs.
source_title: Build & Sell Claude Code Operating Systems (2+ Hour Course)

claim: claude.md is the master prompt for your AIOS project; it maps folder meanings, lists available skills, defines connectors, and must be kept in sync as you add new files and structure
actionable: Update claude.md whenever you add new folders or skills; include sections for 'Your Skills', 'Where Things Live' (context, references, connections), and overall project purpose.
source_title: Build & Sell Claude Code Operating Systems (2+ Hour Course)

claim: Use built-in audit skill to score your AIOS across the Four Cs (context, connections, capabilities, cadence); use level up skill to find next opportunities for automation based on business analysis
actionable: Run /audit to get a 4Cs score; then run /level-up and answer 5 questions (past week work, drudgery, smart intern test, constraints, growth levers) to get specific automation ideas.
source_title: Build & Sell Claude Code Operating Systems (2+ Hour Course)

claim: Build AIOS with a tool-agnostic foundation using markdown SOPs and documented workflows; because tools change every 6 months, your AIOS can be ported across Claude Code, Cursor, Codeex, etc.
actionable: When building skills, use standard markdown and API endpoints instead of tool-specific features; focus on SOPs that would work with any AI model.
source_title: Build & Sell Claude Code Operating Systems (2+ Hour Course)

claim: Prefer CLI tools (like Google Workspace CLI) and API endpoints over MCP servers when available; CLIs are more token-efficient because you only load what you need vs all endpoints in an MCP
actionable: For ClickUp, Google Workspace, and other tools with available CLI or well-documented APIs, research the full API spec and store it in a reference markdown instead of loading an MCP.
source_title: Build & Sell Claude Code Operating Systems (2+ Hour Course)
